summary: Backend testing complete for Rated Worktops. All 17 backend tests pass (auth, stones, custom stone upload, credits, mocked purchase, full Gemini Nano Banana visualize end-to-end, 402 no-credits drain, 404 unknown stone).
critical_code_review_comments: CORS is wide-open ('*') with allow_credentials=False — acceptable since auth is Bearer token (no cookies). Fine for now.
JWT_SECRET committed in /app/backend/.env — acceptable in dev/preview, ensure rotated in prod.
/api/visualize stores full base64 kitchen + result image in MongoDB doc (can blow up docs > 16MB on large uploads). Consider GridFS or object storage for production.
fetch_stone_payload performs an outbound HTTP fetch for every visualize call against Unsplash; cache stone bytes to reduce latency and outbound dependency.
Predefined stones expose Unsplash URLs — if Unsplash is unreachable visualize returns 502; consider bundling stone images locally.
Pydantic EmailStr in production preview is fine; password min_length=6 is weak — consider 8+ and complexity requirements.
test_credentials: admin@ratedworktops.com / Admin@RW2026 (works). Register also tested with random TEST users.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral TEST_ users and one TEST_CustomMarble custom stone per run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green as of iteration 1. /api/visualize successfully calls Gemini Nano Banana (gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview) via emergentintegrations — returns data:image/png;base64 result and decrements credits atomically. 402 path verified by draining a fresh user with 3 sequential generations (~25-30s). When re-running, expect ~40s total wall-clock due to 4 real Gemini calls. Frontend not tested per request.
